treatments.name: Lenvatinib (First-line TKI)

evidence_level.rationale: Phase 3 SELECT trial (N=392) published in NEJM; definitive RCT establishing lenvatinib as first-line standard for RAI-refractory DTC

survival_benefit.rationale: Median PFS 18.3 vs 3.6 months (HR 0.21, p<0.001); ORR 64.8% vs 1.5%; median OS 43.2 months in treatment arm; among responders OS 52.2 months

accessibility.rationale: FDA approved (2015) for RAI-refractory DTC; globally available; NCCN Category 1 recommendation

safety_profile.rationale: Significant toxicity requiring dose reductions in ~67% of patients; common AEs include hypertension (68%), diarrhea (59%), fatigue (59%), decreased appetite (50%), weight loss (46%), proteinuria; manageable with dose modifications

biomarker_match.rationale: No biomarker requirement; effective across all molecular subtypes of RAI-refractory DTC including NRAS-mutated and TERT-mutated tumors

treatments.mechanism_of_action: Multi-kinase inhibitor targeting VEGFR1-3, FGFR1-4, PDGFRalpha, RET, and KIT. Inhibits tumor angiogenesis and directly suppresses tumor cell proliferation through blockade of multiple oncogenic signaling pathways.

key_evidence.study_name: SELECT (Study of (E7080) LEnvatinib in Differentiated Cancer of the Thyroid)

biomarker_requirements: None — approved for all RAI-refractory DTC regardless of mutation status

treatments.name: Cabozantinib (Second-line after VEGFR-TKI)

evidence_level.rationale: Phase 3 COSMIC-311 trial (N=258) with significant PFS benefit; led to FDA approval in September 2021 for post-VEGFR-TKI RAI-refractory DTC

survival_benefit.rationale: Median PFS 11.0 vs 1.9 months (HR 0.22, p<0.0001); ORR 11% including 1 CR; median OS 19.4 months; designed as second-line therapy after lenvatinib/sorafenib progression

accessibility.rationale: FDA approved (2021) for DTC progressed after prior VEGFR-targeted therapy and RAI-refractory; available in most countries; requires prior TKI failure for approved indication

safety_profile.rationale: Grade 3/4 AEs in 62% of patients; 67% required dose reductions; median average daily dose only 39.5mg of 60mg starting dose; common: hypertension (12%), PPE (10%), fatigue (9%)

biomarker_match.rationale: No specific biomarker required; targets MET, VEGFR2, RET, and AXL; additional MET/AXL targeting may provide activity in NRAS-driven tumors through alternative pathway inhibition

treatments.mechanism_of_action: Multi-kinase inhibitor targeting MET, VEGFR2, AXL, RET, KIT, and FLT3. Unique MET and AXL inhibition differentiates it from lenvatinib/sorafenib and may overcome acquired resistance mechanisms. Also has activity against bone microenvironment through MET inhibition.
biomarker_requirements: Prior VEGFR-targeted therapy required for approved indication

treatments.name: Sorafenib (Alternative First-line TKI)

evidence_level.rationale: Phase 3 DECISION trial (N=417) published in Lancet; first TKI approved for RAI-refractory DTC; definitive randomized evidence

survival_benefit.rationale: Median PFS 10.8 vs 5.8 months (HR 0.59, p<0.0001); inferior to lenvatinib PFS (10.8 vs 18.3 months); no OS benefit demonstrated due to high crossover rate (>75%); ORR only 12.2%

accessibility.rationale: FDA approved (2013) for RAI-refractory DTC; first approved agent; widely available globally; NCCN Category 1 recommendation

safety_profile.rationale: High toxicity burden: hand-foot skin reaction (76.3%), diarrhea (68.6%), alopecia (67.1%), rash (50.2%); 98.6% of patients experienced AEs; dose reductions frequently required

biomarker_match.rationale: No biomarker required; modest enhanced PFS in BRAF-mutant patients (20.5 vs 9.4 months) but less specific molecular targeting for NRAS mutations compared to MEK inhibitors

treatments.mechanism_of_action: Multi-kinase inhibitor targeting RAF (including CRAF), VEGFR1-3, PDGFR-beta, RET, FLT3, and KIT. Inhibits tumor angiogenesis and proliferation through blockade of the RAF/MEK/ERK pathway and receptor tyrosine kinases.

key_evidence.study_name: DECISION (StuDy of sorafEnib in loCally advanced or metastatIc patientS with radioactive Iodine refractory thyrOid caNcer)

biomarker_requirements: None — approved for all RAI-refractory DTC

treatments.name: Denosumab (Bone-targeted Agent)

evidence_level.rationale: Phase 3 RCTs in multiple solid tumor bone metastases; meta-analyses demonstrating superiority over zoledronic acid for SRE prevention; evidence extrapolated from broad solid tumor populations

survival_benefit.rationale: Does not extend OS but significantly delays skeletal-related events (pathologic fractures, spinal cord compression, need for surgery/radiation); critical for quality of life and functional status in bone-metastatic disease

accessibility.rationale: FDA approved for prevention of SREs in patients with bone metastases from solid tumors (Xgeva 120mg Q4W); widely available; no renal dose adjustment needed unlike zoledronic acid

safety_profile.rationale: Generally well-tolerated; key risks include osteonecrosis of jaw (1-2%), hypocalcemia (requires calcium/vitamin D supplementation), and atypical femoral fractures; better renal safety profile than bisphosphonates

biomarker_match.rationale: Universally applicable for bone metastases regardless of molecular profile; RANKL pathway inhibition directly addresses osteolytic bone destruction common in follicular thyroid cancer bone metastases

treatments.mechanism_of_action: Fully human monoclonal antibody against RANKL (receptor activator of nuclear factor kappa-B ligand). Inhibits osteoclast-mediated bone resorption, preventing skeletal-related events including pathologic fractures, spinal cord compression, and hypercalcemia in patients with bone metastases.

key_evidence.study_name: Integrated Phase 3 RCTs (Denosumab vs Zoledronic Acid in Solid Tumors)

biomarker_requirements: Bone metastases from solid tumor confirmed on imaging
Adequate calcium and vitamin D levels prior to initiation

treatments.name: Zoledronic Acid (Bone-targeted Agent)

evidence_level.rationale: Prospective evaluation in DTC bone metastases (N=19); extensive Phase 3 data in solid tumor bone mets; established standard for SRE prevention alongside denosumab

survival_benefit.rationale: Reduces SREs but no OS benefit; delays pathologic fracture, spinal cord compression, and hypercalcemia; modestly inferior to denosumab for SRE prevention (RR 0.84 favoring denosumab)

accessibility.rationale: FDA approved; generic available; administered as IV infusion Q3-4 weeks; lower cost than denosumab; widely available globally

safety_profile.rationale: Renal toxicity requires creatinine monitoring and dose adjustment; acute phase reaction (flu-like symptoms) common after first infusion; ONJ risk similar to denosumab; contraindicated with CrCl <35 mL/min

biomarker_match.rationale: Universally applicable for bone metastases regardless of molecular profile; directly addresses osteolytic bone destruction in follicular thyroid cancer

treatments.mechanism_of_action: Nitrogen-containing bisphosphonate that inhibits farnesyl pyrophosphate synthase in osteoclasts, inducing osteoclast apoptosis and reducing bone resorption. Also has direct anti-tumor effects in preclinical models through inhibition of tumor cell adhesion to bone matrix.

key_evidence.study_name: Prospective Evaluation of Zoledronic Acid in Bone Metastases from DTC

biomarker_requirements: Calcium and vitamin D supplementation required
notable_side_effects: Acute phase reaction (fever, myalgia, arthralgia after first dose)

treatments.name: SBRT (Stereotactic Body Radiation Therapy) for Bone Metastases

evidence_level.rationale: Multiple systematic reviews and meta-analyses; no Phase 3 RCTs specific to thyroid cancer bone mets but strong evidence from mixed solid tumor populations; high local control rates documented

survival_benefit.rationale: Local control rate 88% at 2 years for thyroid cancer mets; 80% pain response rate; complete pain response 36%; delays SREs; critical for preventing pathologic fractures in weight-bearing bone mets

accessibility.rationale: Available at most academic and large community cancer centers with stereotactic capability; requires specialized treatment planning; covered by insurance for bone metastases

safety_profile.rationale: Very low toxicity: grade 3-5 toxicity rate only 0.5%; pathologic fracture risk 3.1% post-SBRT; well-tolerated with 1-5 fraction courses; superior to conventional EBRT for radioresistant thyroid histology

biomarker_match.rationale: Particularly advantageous for thyroid cancer given its moderate radioresistance — SBRT's ablative doses overcome radioresistance better than conventional fractionation; universally applicable regardless of molecular markers

treatments.mechanism_of_action: Delivers highly focused, ablative radiation doses (typically 24-30 Gy in 1-5 fractions) to bone metastases with millimeter precision, causing direct tumor cell DNA damage and vascular disruption. Ablative doses overcome the relative radioresistance of thyroid cancer compared to conventional fractionated EBRT.

key_evidence.study_name: Systematic Review: SBRT for Bone Metastases (Mixed Solid Tumors including Thyroid)

biomarker_requirements: None — applicable to all bone metastases
Adequate distance from critical structures (spinal cord, bowel)

treatments.name: Conventional External Beam Radiation Therapy (EBRT) for Bone Palliation

evidence_level.rationale: Extensive Phase 3 evidence for palliative radiation of bone metastases; ASTRO clinical practice guidelines published 2024; decades of established use

survival_benefit.rationale: Palliative intent only — 60-80% pain response rate, 25-30% complete pain response; does not extend OS; thyroid cancer is moderately radioresistant limiting local control vs SBRT

accessibility.rationale: Universally available at all radiation oncology centers; multiple fractionation options (single 8 Gy to 30 Gy/10 fractions); covered by insurance; rapid scheduling possible for urgent palliation

safety_profile.rationale: Very well tolerated; minimal acute toxicity; brief treatment course; no systemic effects; suitable for patients with poor performance status or comorbidities

biomarker_match.rationale: No biomarker requirement; universally applicable; however, thyroid cancer's moderate radioresistance means conventional doses may be suboptimal compared to SBRT

treatments.mechanism_of_action: Delivers fractionated radiation doses (8-30 Gy) to bone metastases for pain palliation, local tumor control, and prevention of pathologic fractures. Acts through direct DNA damage and indirect free radical damage to tumor cells and tumor-associated vasculature.

key_evidence.study_name: ASTRO Clinical Practice Guideline: EBRT for Palliation of Bone Metastases

biomarker_requirements: None — universally applicable for symptomatic bone metastases
notable_side_effects: Nausea (if treating vertebral body near bowel)

treatments.name: Selumetinib Redifferentiation + Radioiodine Therapy

evidence_level.rationale: Phase 2 pilot study (N=20) published in NEJM; landmark proof-of-concept study but small sample size; ASTRA Phase 3 trial in adjuvant setting did not meet primary endpoint

survival_benefit.rationale: 100% of NRAS-mutant patients (5/5) achieved restored RAI uptake; 5/8 treated patients had partial response; mean thyroglobulin reduction 89%; potential for durable disease control via reinstated RAI therapy

accessibility.rationale: Not FDA approved for this indication; selumetinib approved for NF1 (Koselugo) but off-label for thyroid redifferentiation; requires specialized nuclear medicine center for I-124 dosimetry

safety_profile.rationale: No grade 3+ toxicity attributed to selumetinib in the pilot study; common grade 1-2 AEs: fatigue (80%), rash (70%), elevated liver enzymes (70%); well-tolerated 4-week course

biomarker_match.rationale: Exceptional match for NRAS Q61R: 100% of NRAS-mutant patients achieved dosimetry threshold for RAI therapy vs only 1/9 BRAF-mutant patients; NRAS mutations predict superior response to MEK inhibitor-mediated redifferentiation

treatments.mechanism_of_action: Selective MEK1/2 inhibitor that suppresses hyperactive MAPK signaling caused by NRAS Q61R mutation, restoring expression of sodium-iodide symporter (NIS) and thyroid peroxidase (TPO), thereby re-enabling radioiodine uptake and allowing therapeutic I-131 administration.

key_evidence.study_name: Selumetinib-Enhanced Radioiodine Uptake in Advanced Thyroid Cancer

biomarker_requirements: NRAS Q61R mutation (strongly preferred — 100% RAI uptake restoration)

treatments.name: Lenvatinib + Pembrolizumab Combination

evidence_level.rationale: Phase 2 ITOG trial with two cohorts; ORR 65.5% in lenvatinib-naive patients; median PFS 26.8 months; not yet confirmed in Phase 3 RCT

survival_benefit.rationale: Median PFS 26.8 months in lenvatinib-naive cohort (superior to lenvatinib monotherapy PFS 18.3 months); 12-month PFS 72%; may enhance durability of TKI response through immune activation

accessibility.rationale: Both drugs individually FDA approved but combination not approved for DTC; available off-label at academic centers; clinical trials ongoing; cost of dual therapy is substantial

safety_profile.rationale: Additive toxicity from dual therapy; immune-related AEs from pembrolizumab added to lenvatinib TKI toxicities; thyroid dysfunction may be complex in thyroidectomized patients on TSH suppression

biomarker_match.rationale: No specific biomarker required; PD-L1 status did not predict response in KEYNOTE-158; TERT-mutated tumors may have higher tumor mutational burden potentially enhancing immunotherapy response

treatments.mechanism_of_action: Combines lenvatinib's multi-kinase inhibition (VEGFR, FGFR, RET) with pembrolizumab's PD-1 immune checkpoint blockade. Lenvatinib's antiangiogenic effects may enhance tumor immune infiltration and synergize with anti-PD-1 immunotherapy by reversing immunosuppressive tumor microenvironment.

key_evidence.study_name: ITOG Phase II: Lenvatinib + Pembrolizumab in RAIR-DTC

treatments.name: Trametinib Redifferentiation + Radioiodine Therapy

evidence_level.rationale: MERAIODE Phase 2 trial (N=11 RAS-mutated); multiple small prospective studies and real-world cohorts confirm feasibility; growing but still limited evidence base

survival_benefit.rationale: Two-thirds of RAS-mutated tumors showed increased I-131 uptake; 6-month response rate 20% in MERAIODE; real-world data shows most NRAS Q61R patients achieve RAI uptake restoration after 6 weeks of trametinib

accessibility.rationale: Trametinib (Mekinist) is FDA-approved for melanoma and ATC (with dabrafenib); off-label use for DTC redifferentiation requires institutional protocol; more widely available than selumetinib

safety_profile.rationale: Known MEK inhibitor class toxicity profile; manageable AEs including rash, diarrhea, peripheral edema; short 6-week treatment course limits cumulative toxicity

biomarker_match.rationale: Directly targets the MAPK pathway downstream of NRAS Q61R; trametinib is the preferred MEK inhibitor for NRAS-mutant redifferentiation per current ITOG consensus; excellent molecular match

treatments.mechanism_of_action: Selective MEK1/2 inhibitor that blocks constitutive MAPK pathway activation driven by NRAS Q61R mutation. Six-week treatment course restores thyroid differentiation gene expression (NIS, TPO, thyroglobulin), re-enabling therapeutic radioiodine uptake in previously RAI-refractory lesions.
biomarker_requirements: Access to I-124 or I-131 dosimetry scanning

treatments.name: Pazopanib

evidence_level.rationale: Phase 2 trials (consortium study N=37, international study); ORR 49% with 66% probability of response lasting >1 year; PAZOTHYR trial evaluated intermittent vs continuous dosing

survival_benefit.rationale: ORR 49% with durable responses (66% lasting >1 year); median PFS approximately 11.7 months; comparable to sorafenib PFS; provides alternative after lenvatinib/sorafenib failure

accessibility.rationale: FDA approved for renal cell carcinoma and soft tissue sarcoma but NOT for thyroid cancer; off-label use possible; available as oral medication

safety_profile.rationale: Significant hepatotoxicity risk requiring liver function monitoring; hypertension, diarrhea, hair color changes common; hepatic failure reported rarely; manageable with monitoring

biomarker_match.rationale: No specific biomarker requirement; VEGFR/PDGFR/KIT inhibitor with broad anti-angiogenic activity; response not robustly predicted by mutational status in clinical trials

treatments.mechanism_of_action: Multi-kinase inhibitor targeting VEGFR1-3, PDGFR-alpha/beta, and c-KIT. Suppresses tumor angiogenesis and disrupts tumor-stromal signaling. Provides an alternative anti-angiogenic approach after progression on lenvatinib or sorafenib.

key_evidence.study_name: Phase 2 Consortium Study: Pazopanib in Progressive RAI-Refractory DTC

treatments.name: Pembrolizumab Monotherapy

evidence_level.rationale: Phase 2 KEYNOTE-158 study (N=103 DTC patients); also Phase 1b KEYNOTE-028 data; well-powered but modest response rates in DTC

survival_benefit.rationale: ORR only 6.8% (7/103 patients); durable responses in responders but low response rate; limited activity as monotherapy in differentiated thyroid cancer; may be more useful in combination

accessibility.rationale: FDA approved for multiple indications and MSI-H/TMB-H tumors; off-label for DTC; widely available; IV administration Q3W or Q6W

safety_profile.rationale: Well-characterized immune-related AE profile; manageable with corticosteroids; thyroid dysfunction common but already thyroidectomized; risk of pneumonitis, hepatitis, colitis

biomarker_match.rationale: PD-L1 status not predictive of response in DTC; TERT promoter mutation may correlate with higher TMB but relationship to checkpoint inhibitor response in DTC is not established

treatments.mechanism_of_action: Humanized monoclonal antibody targeting PD-1 receptor on T cells, blocking interaction with PD-L1/PD-L2 ligands and restoring anti-tumor immune surveillance. Releases immune checkpoint-mediated inhibition of cytotoxic T lymphocytes.
biomarker_requirements: PD-L1 status (not predictive but often assessed)
No specific thyroid cancer biomarker requirement
treatments.availability: Off-label for DTC (FDA approved for multiple other indications)

treatments.name: Axitinib

evidence_level.rationale: Phase 2 data in RAI-refractory DTC; ORR 41% overall, 69% in first-line; limited sample sizes; not studied in Phase 3 for thyroid cancer

survival_benefit.rationale: ORR 41% overall, 69% in first-line setting; represents alternative VEGFR-TKI option; no head-to-head comparison with lenvatinib or sorafenib

accessibility.rationale: FDA approved for renal cell carcinoma but NOT for thyroid cancer; off-label use possible; oral administration; may be used when other TKIs poorly tolerated

safety_profile.rationale: VEGFR-TKI class toxicities; hypertension, diarrhea, fatigue; potentially better tolerated than lenvatinib due to more selective VEGFR targeting

biomarker_match.rationale: No specific biomarker requirement; potent selective VEGFR1-3 inhibitor; applicable across molecular subtypes

treatments.mechanism_of_action: Potent and selective VEGFR1-3 inhibitor with additional activity against PDGFR and c-KIT. More selective VEGFR inhibition profile compared to lenvatinib or sorafenib, potentially offering a different toxicity profile while maintaining anti-angiogenic efficacy.

key_evidence.study_name: Phase 2 Axitinib in Advanced RAI-resistant DTC and MTC

treatments.name: Avutometinib + Defactinib (RAF/MEK clamp + FAK inhibitor)

evidence_level.rationale: Phase 1 FRAME trial completed in solid tumors; Phase 2 study at MSK (NCT06007924) recruiting for thyroid cancer; no thyroid-specific efficacy data yet published

survival_benefit.rationale: Preclinical rationale strong for RAF-dimer-driven thyroid cancers; encouraging early Phase 1 responses in solid tumors; NRAS Q61R signals through RAF dimers making this mechanistically relevant; no thyroid-specific survival data

accessibility.rationale: Available only through clinical trial at MSK and select sites; avutometinib+defactinib FDA approved for LGSOC (2025) but not thyroid cancer; very limited access currently

safety_profile.rationale: Phase 1 FRAME trial showed manageable safety profile; avutometinib dosed at 3.2mg BIW + defactinib 200mg BID; GI toxicity and fatigue most common; acceptable for exploration

biomarker_match.rationale: NRAS Q61R drives RAF dimer signaling — direct target of avutometinib's RAF/MEK clamp mechanism; FAK inhibition by defactinib may overcome adaptive resistance; strong molecular rationale for this mutation

treatments.mechanism_of_action: Avutometinib is a RAF/MEK clamp inhibitor that uniquely blocks both RAF dimer-dependent and MEK-dependent signaling, particularly effective against NRAS-driven tumors. Defactinib inhibits focal adhesion kinase (FAK), blocking a key adaptive resistance pathway and enhancing anti-tumor immune responses.

key_evidence.study_name: Phase 2 Study of Avutometinib and Defactinib in Thyroid Cancer (NCT06007924)

biomarker_requirements: Trial enrollment at MSK or select sites

treatments.name: Zanzalintinib (XL092) — Next-generation Cabozantinib

evidence_level.rationale: Phase 2 trial ongoing for RAI-refractory DTC; successor compound to cabozantinib with improved pharmacological properties; no published efficacy data yet in thyroid cancer

survival_benefit.rationale: Theoretical improvement over cabozantinib based on enhanced target engagement; PFS at 12 months is primary endpoint; no mature data available

accessibility.rationale: Available only through active clinical trial; not yet FDA approved for any indication; enrollment may be limited to select centers

safety_profile.rationale: Expected similar class effect to cabozantinib but potentially improved safety profile due to optimized selectivity; early safety data from other tumor types appears manageable

biomarker_match.rationale: Targets MET, VEGFR, and additional kinases; applicable across molecular subtypes; may have enhanced activity versus cabozantinib in certain molecular contexts

treatments.mechanism_of_action: Next-generation multi-kinase inhibitor targeting MET, VEGFR, and other kinases with improved selectivity profile compared to cabozantinib. Designed to enhance target engagement and potentially improve the therapeutic index in RAI-refractory DTC.

key_evidence.study_name: Phase II Study of XL092 (Zanzalintinib) in RAI-Refractory DTC

clinical_trials.title: Phase 2 Study of Avutometinib and Defactinib in People With Thyroid Cancer
sites: Memorial Sloan Kettering Cancer Center, New York, USA
clinical_trials.early_results: Phase 1 FRAME trial showed manageable safety and encouraging responses in solid tumors; thyroid-specific data pending enrollment completion (target N=30)

clinical_trials.title: MERAIODE: Redifferentiation Phase II Trial with Trametinib (RAS) or Dabrafenib+Trametinib (BRAF) Followed by RAI
biomarker_requirements: RAS mutation (NRAS/KRAS/HRAS) or BRAF V600E mutation
clinical_trials.early_results: RAS-mutated cohort (N=11): 2/3 of tumors showed increased I-131 uptake; 6-month response rate 20%; trametinib monotherapy for 6 weeks before RAI administration

clinical_trials.title: Study of Pembrolizumab in Participants With Advanced Solid Tumors (DTC cohort)
clinical_trials.early_results: DTC cohort (N=103): ORR 6.8% (7 responses); durable responses observed in responders; manageable safety profile; modest monotherapy activity in DTC

clinical_trials.title: Lenvatinib + Pembrolizumab in Progressive RAI-Refractory DTC
sites: International Thyroid Oncology Group — multi-center, international
clinical_trials.early_results: Cohort 1 (lenvatinib-naive, N=30): ORR 65.5%, median PFS 26.8 months, 12-month PFS 72%; Cohort 2 (lenvatinib-pretreated, N=27): ORR 16%, median PFS 10.0 months

clinical_trials.title: Phase II Study of Zanzalintinib (XL092) in RAI-Refractory DTC
clinical_trials.early_results: No mature data; PFS at 12 months is primary endpoint; next-generation MET/VEGFR inhibitor with potentially improved therapeutic index over cabozantinib

clinical_trials.title: Redifferentiation Therapy with MEK Inhibitors in NRAS-mutant RAI-Refractory DTC — ITOG Consensus Protocol
sites: Memorial Sloan Kettering Cancer Center, New York
biomarker_requirements: NRAS Q61R/K/L mutation confirmed by NGS
clinical_trials.early_results: Real-world data: All but one patient with NRAS Q61R mutation achieved RAI uptake restoration after 6 weeks of trametinib; excellent interim response rates reported at ASCO 2023

combination_strategies.rationale: Lenvatinib's anti-angiogenic effects enhance tumor immune infiltration and reverse immunosuppressive microenvironment. Combination achieved ORR 65.5% and median PFS 26.8 months in lenvatinib-naive patients, suggesting enhanced durability over lenvatinib monotherapy (PFS 18.3 months in SELECT).

Six-week trametinib course restores NIS expression and RAI uptake in NRAS Q61R-mutated tumors by suppressing hyperactive MAPK signaling. Enables re-application of I-131 therapy in previously RAI-refractory disease. 100% of NRAS-mutant patients showed RAI uptake restoration with selumetinib in prior study, providing strong mechanistic rationale.

Multimodal approach: systemic TKI controls overall disease burden while denosumab prevents skeletal-related events and SBRT provides durable local control of painful or structurally threatening bone lesions. Addresses the complementary goals of systemic disease control and bone-specific morbidity prevention in M1 bone disease.

NRAS Q61R drives signaling through RAF dimers — avutometinib uniquely clamps RAF/MEK interaction to block this pathway. Defactinib inhibits FAK-mediated adaptive resistance and may enhance immune responses. Dual targeting addresses resistance mechanisms observed with single-agent MEK inhibitors in RAS-driven tumors.

Cabozantinib's MET/AXL inhibition may reverse immunosuppressive tumor microenvironment and synergize with PD-1 blockade. MET pathway activation is implicated in immune evasion. Combination being explored in other solid tumors with encouraging signals.

supportive_care.evidence: NCCN/ATA guidelines; retrospective analyses showing improved PFS with TSH suppression in metastatic DTC; recommended TSH <0.1 mIU/L for high-risk/metastatic disease
supportive_care.benefit: Suppression of TSH-driven tumor growth; high-risk/metastatic DTC requires TSH <0.1 mIU/L to minimize growth stimulus on residual thyroid cancer cells expressing TSH receptors; must balance against osteoporosis risk in setting of bone metastases

supportive_care.evidence: Required with both denosumab and zoledronic acid to prevent treatment-induced hypocalcemia; especially critical in thyroidectomized patients who may have hypoparathyroidism
supportive_care.benefit: Prevents hypocalcemia from bone-modifying agents; supports bone integrity; mandatory supplementation with denosumab (calcium 500mg BID + vitamin D 400IU daily minimum); monitor corrected calcium levels regularly

supportive_care.approach: Physical Therapy and Fall Prevention for Bone Metastases
supportive_care.evidence: ASCO guidelines on bone health in cancer; weight-bearing exercise programs shown to improve bone density and reduce fracture risk in cancer patients; physical therapy assessment for pathologic fracture risk
supportive_care.benefit: Reduces pathologic fracture risk through strengthening of supporting musculature; improves functional status and quality of life; biomechanical assessment identifies high-risk lesions requiring prophylactic stabilization; 30-50% improvement in functional outcomes

supportive_care.approach: Orthopedic Surgery Consultation for Impending Pathologic Fractures
supportive_care.evidence: Mirels scoring system for pathologic fracture risk; prospective data supporting prophylactic stabilization when Mirels score >=9; NCCN bone metastasis management guidelines
supportive_care.benefit: Prophylactic stabilization of weight-bearing bones with impending fracture (Mirels score >=9) prevents catastrophic pathologic fractures; improves quality of life and functional independence; should be assessed for all bone metastases in weight-bearing locations

supportive_care.approach: Pain Management — WHO Analgesic Ladder + Interventional Approaches
supportive_care.evidence: WHO analgesic ladder; ASCO guidelines on cancer pain management; evidence for NSAID efficacy in bone pain; interventional options including nerve blocks and radiofrequency ablation
supportive_care.benefit: Multimodal pain control for bone metastasis-related pain: NSAIDs for prostaglandin-mediated bone pain, opioids as needed, adjuvant agents (gabapentin for neuropathic component); interventional procedures for refractory pain; SBRT for pain in radioresistant thyroid histology

supportive_care.evidence: Meta-analyses showing exercise improves cancer-related fatigue, quality of life, and potentially survival in metastatic cancer patients; protein optimization for bone health
supportive_care.benefit: Resistance exercise 2-3x weekly improves fatigue, functional capacity, and bone density; adequate protein intake (1.2-1.5 g/kg/day) supports musculoskeletal health; may mitigate TKI-related weight loss and sarcopenia common with lenvatinib
